pageInfo.pagePurpose: To create a new sales order by entering customer information, item details, and other relevant sales information.

clickableElements.label: Navigate To (Opt+0)
clickableElements.businessIntent: To open a quick navigation or search menu for accessing different parts of the application.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: A search bar or navigation modal appears on the screen.

clickableElements.label: Items
clickableElements.businessIntent: To expand the 'Items' section of the main navigation menu.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: A sub-menu with links related to items and price lists is displayed.

clickableElements.label: Inventory
clickableElements.businessIntent: To expand the 'Inventory' section of the main navigation menu.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: A sub-menu with links related to inventory is displayed.

clickableElements.label: Sales
clickableElements.businessIntent: To expand the 'Sales' section of the main navigation menu.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: A sub-menu with links related to sales activities like customers, quotes, and invoices is displayed.

clickableElements.label: Purchases
clickableElements.businessIntent: To expand the 'Purchases' section of the main navigation menu.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: A sub-menu with links related to purchasing activities is displayed.

clickableElements.label: Time Tracking
clickableElements.businessIntent: To expand the 'Time Tracking' section of the main navigation menu.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: A sub-menu with links related to projects and timesheets is displayed.

clickableElements.label: Accountant
clickableElements.businessIntent: To expand the 'Accountant' section of the main navigation menu.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: A sub-menu with links related to accounting functions is displayed.

clickableElements.label: Bulk Actions
clickableElements.businessIntent: To perform an action on multiple selected line items in the sales order.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: A dropdown or menu with available bulk actions appears.

clickableElements.label: Add Items in Bulk
clickableElements.businessIntent: To add multiple items to the sales order at once.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: A modal or a new interface opens, allowing the user to select multiple items to add to the order.

clickableElements.label: Save as Draft
clickableElements.businessIntent: To save the current sales order without finalizing or sending it.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The sales order data is saved in a draft state, and the user is likely redirected to the sales order list page.

clickableElements.label: Save and Send
clickableElements.businessIntent: To finalize the sales order and send it to the customer.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The sales order is saved and a workflow to send it (likely via email) is initiated. The user may be shown a confirmation or redirected.

clickableElements.label: Cancel
clickableElements.businessIntent: To discard the new sales order and exit the creation form.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The form is cleared, and the user is redirected to the previous page, likely the sales order list.

clickableElements.label: Home
clickableElements.businessIntent: To navigate to the application's main dashboard or home page.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The user is taken to the home page.

clickableElements.label: Items
clickableElements.businessIntent: To navigate to the list of all items.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The user is taken to the items index page.

clickableElements.label: Price Lists
clickableElements.businessIntent: To navigate to the list of price lists.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The user is taken to the price lists page.

clickableElements.label: Inventory Adjustments
clickableElements.businessIntent: To navigate to the list of inventory adjustments.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The user is taken to the inventory adjustments page.

clickableElements.label: Customers
clickableElements.businessIntent: To navigate to the list of customers.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The user is taken to the customer contacts page.

clickableElements.label: Quotes
clickableElements.businessIntent: To navigate to the list of quotes.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The user is taken to the quotes list page.

clickableElements.label: Sales Orders
clickableElements.businessIntent: To navigate to the list of sales orders.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The user is taken to the sales orders list page.

clickableElements.label: Invoices
clickableElements.businessIntent: To navigate to the list of invoices.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The user is taken to the invoices list page.

clickableElements.label: Add New Row
clickableElements.businessIntent: To add a new empty line item to the items table in the sales order.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: A new row appears in the item details table, allowing the user to add another product or service.

clickableElements.label: Register Now
clickableElements.businessIntent: To register for a product webinar.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The user is taken to an external webinar registration page.

clickableElements.label: Create a channel
clickableElements.businessIntent: To create a new channel in an integrated chat application.
clickableElements.expectedOutcome: The user is taken to an external chat application page to create a channel.

tables.purpose: To list the products or services being sold in the sales order, including their quantities, rates, and total amounts.

columns.name: Drag item up or down
columns.meaning: A control to reorder the line items.
columns.businessPurpose: Allows the user to change the sequence in which items appear on the sales order document.

columns.name: Item Details
columns.meaning: The name or description of the product or service.
columns.businessPurpose: To identify the specific item being sold.

columns.name: Quantity
columns.meaning: The number of units of the item being sold.
columns.businessPurpose: To specify the volume of the item for pricing and inventory tracking.

columns.name: Rate
columns.meaning: The price per single unit of the item.
columns.businessPurpose: To define the unit cost for calculating the total amount.

columns.name: Amount
columns.meaning: The total cost for the line item (Quantity x Rate).
columns.businessPurpose: To show the subtotal for each line item on the sales order.

forms.purpose: To capture all the necessary details to create a new sales order.

fields.businessMeaning: The name of the customer for whom the sales order is being created.
A unique identifier for the sales order, likely auto-generated.
An optional reference number, such as a customer's purchase order number.
The date the sales order was created.
The date when the items are expected to be shipped to the customer.
The agreed-upon terms for payment (e.g., Due on Receipt, Net 30).
The method by which the goods will be delivered to the customer.
The name of the salesperson associated with this sale.
A field to select a specific price list to apply to the items in this sales order.
A field for entering notes that will be visible to the customer on the sales order document.
The discount amount or percentage to be applied to the subtotal of the sales order.
An option to apply Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) to the total amount.
An option to apply Tax Collected at Source (TCS) to the total amount.
A field for specifying the terms and conditions of the sale, to be displayed on the sales order.
An input to upload and attach files (e.g., contracts, specifications) to the sales order.

workflows.name: Create and Save Sales Order Draft
steps: User fills in customer and sales order details.
User adds one or more line items to the item table.
User clicks the 'Save as Draft' button.

workflows.name: Create and Send Sales Order
steps: User fills in all required customer and sales order details.
User adds one or more line items to the item table.
User clicks the 'Save and Send' button.

workflows.name: Add Items to Sales Order
steps: User clicks in the 'Item Details' field or clicks 'Add New Row'.
User selects an item from a list.
User enters quantity and confirms the rate.
The system calculates the line item amount.

userGoals.name: Create a formal sales order
userGoals.description: To create a new sales order document for a customer transaction.

userGoals.name: Define items and pricing for a sale
userGoals.description: To specify the exact products or services, quantities, and prices for a particular sale.

userGoals.name: Save an incomplete order for later
userGoals.description: To save a partially completed sales order as a draft to be finished at a later time.

userGoals.name: Quickly navigate to other related business functions
userGoals.description: To move from creating a sales order to other areas like customer management, inventory, or invoicing.

predictedMissingUI.reason: The 'Customer Name' label and 'Select or add a customer' text in the HTML imply a mechanism to search for and select an existing customer or add a new one, which is not fully detailed in the facts.
The 'Item Details' field in the table with the placeholder 'Type or click to select an item.' indicates an interactive element for searching and selecting products from a catalog.
The 'Add Items in Bulk' button strongly suggests a corresponding modal or screen where users can view a list of items and select multiple to add to the order simultaneously.
The 'Save and Send' button typically precedes a confirmation step in SaaS applications, where the user can review the recipient's email and a message before finalizing the send action.

pageNarrative: This page is a form for creating a 'New Sales Order'. A user can specify the customer, dates, and reference numbers for the order. The core of the page is a table for adding line items, where the user selects products or services and defines their quantity and rate. The form automatically calculates subtotals and a final total, accounting for discounts and taxes. Upon completion, the user has the option to save the order as a draft or to save and send it directly to the customer.
